title: QA Core Spec FAIL: failure_algebra.types is empty (MISSING_FAILURE_ALGEBRA)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates MISSING_FAILURE_ALGEBRA: the spec declares failure_algebra.types=[] (empty). The validator detects this and emits MISSING_FAILURE_ALGEBRA. This is the most critical failure mode for a QA spec because failure completeness is the primary advantage of QA over other formal systems (DeepMind/Anthropic specs say what should happen; QA specs say what can fail and how). A spec with no classified failure modes is structurally incomplete — it cannot support deterministic failure replay or invariant diff logging.

validation_checks.details: types=[]; len=0 — empty failure algebra is invalid for a QA spec
QA_relation_1, QA_relation_2 declared; sigma references both — all resolve

fail_ledger.message: failure_algebra.types is empty. A QA spec MUST define at least one classified failure mode. Failure completeness is the primary structural advantage of QA over DeepMind/Anthropic-style specs: QA specs classify not just what should happen but what can fail and why. An empty failure algebra means the spec cannot support deterministic failure replay, invariant diff logging, or cert-level failure documentation.
